name: argparse-interface

metadata.name: Argparse-Interface
metadata.summary: An automatic, terminal based interactive interface for any Python 3 `argparse` command line with keyboard and mouse support.

documentation:
# ArgUI

![ArgUI's Logo](/assets/ArgUILogo_transparent.png)

An automatic, terminal based interactive interface for any Python 3 `argparse` command line with keyboard and mouse support.

- [ArgUI](#argui)
  - [See It in Action](#see-it-in-action)
  - [Usage](#usage)
    - [Install as a Dependency](#install-as-a-dependency)
    - [Setup Your Argparse](#setup-your-argparse)
    - [Run Your Program](#run-your-program)
    - [Navigation](#navigation)
  - [Development Setup](#development-setup)

---

## See It in Action

![Demo of the features in ArgUI](/assets/ArgUIDemo_small.gif)

Get a feel for the features of ArgUI using the [Demo.py](./argui/Demo.py) code included in this project.

## Usage

### Install as a Dependency

1. Clone this repo to the desired location.
1. Access this directory with your desired environment.
1. Install this repo as a package: `pip install -e .`

A PyPi release may come in the future.

### Setup Your Argparse

ArgUI supports wrapping any implementation of the Python 3 `argparse` native library.
This will all you to use both standard terminal and interface modes to interact with your program.

```python
# Import
import argparse
import argui

# Setup your ArgumentParser normally
parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="Demo")

# `add_argument`, `add_argument_group`, etc...

# Wrap your parser
interface = argui.Wrapper(parser)

# Get arguments
args: argparse.Namespace = interface.parseArgs()

# `args` is the same as if you had called `parser.parse_args()`
```

See [Demo.py](./argui/Demo.py) for more information.

### Run Your Program

Your program can now be run in both CLI and GUI modes.

To run in CLI mode, simply use your script as normal like `python foo.py -h`.

To run in GUI mode, provide only the `--gui` (by default) argument like `python foo.py --gui`.

### Navigation

Mouse navigation of the GUI is possible in _most_ terminals.

There are known issues with the VSCode terminal on Windows 10 and some others.
However, Mouse navigation does work in Powershell when opened on its own.

Keyboard navigation is always available using `Tab`, `Arrow Keys`, and `Enter`.
But make note that if you are using a terminal within another program (like VSCode), that some more advanced keyboard commands (like `CTRL+S`) may be captured by the container program and not sent to the GUI.

## Development Setup

1. Clone this repo and enter the directory with a terminal.
1. Setup a Python Env: `python -m venv .venv --prompt "argUI"`
1. Enter the Python Env.
1. Install requirements: `pip install -r requirements.txt`

Demo of the `argparse` interface.

functions.name: argui.Demo.getDemoArgParser
documentation: MARK: Functions

functions.name: argui.Demo.runDemo
documentation:
Runs a demonstration of the `argparse` interface with the Argparse Interface attached.

For CLI: `python .\demo.py -bf -m2A 1 -m3A 2 3 foo`

For GUI: `python .\demo.py --gui`

modules.name: argui.Wrapper
documentation: Argparse Interface: Wrapper
Host wrapper for the Interface.
MARK: Imports

classes.name: argui.Wrapper.Wrapper

methods.name: argui.Wrapper.Wrapper.__init__
documentation:
parser: The top-level `ArgumentParser` object to use in the interface.
guiFlag: The flag to use to indicate that the gui should be shown.
guiHelp: The help text to use for the gui flag.
logLevel: The logging level to use.
icon: A single character icon to display in the interface's header or `None`.
debugUI: If `True`, the debug elements will be shown.
Constructor

methods.name: argui.Wrapper.Wrapper.parseArgs
documentation:
Parses the arguments using the method defined by the cli flags.
Will open the gui if prompted.
Otherwise, will parse the cli arguments as normal.

Returns any parsed arguments as an `argparse.Namespace` object or `None` if the gui was quit without submitting.
Functions

methods.name: argui.Wrapper.Wrapper.parseArgsWithGui
documentation:
Explicitly presents the gui (as opposed to the cli or gui) and parses provided arguments.
The gui flag will be ignored.

Returns any parsed arguments as an `argparse.Namespace` object or `None` if the gui was quit without submitting.

methods.name: argui.Wrapper.Wrapper._addGuiArgument
documentation:
Adds the gui Flag argument to the parser.

parser: The parser to add the argument to.
Private Functions

Automatic interface wrapper for the `argparse` module.

Use this class to automatically handle the interface.
MARK: Classes

classes.name: argui.Interface.Interface

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.__init__
documentation:
parser: The top-level `ArgumentParser` object to use in the interface.
guiFlag: The flag used to indicate that the gui should be shown. This will be hidden from the interface.
title: The title of the interface.
subTitle: The subtitle of the interface.
icon: A single character icon to display in the header or `None`.
MARK: Constructor

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.compose
documentation: Defines the interface.
MARK: Lifecycle

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.on_mount
documentation: Run after installing the items in `compose()`.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildNavigatorArea
documentation: Builds the navigator tree.
MARK: UI Builders

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildContentArea
documentation: Builds the input content area.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildParserInterface
documentation:
Yields all UI elements for the given `argparse.ArgumentParser` object chain.
UI elements are added to required and optional sections respecting any subparser or group structures.

parser: The `argparse.ArgumentParser` object to build the UI elements from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildGroupSections
documentation:
Yields all the UI elements for the actions of any given `ParserGroup`.

group: The `ParserGroup` to build the UI elements from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildTabbedGroupSections
documentation: Yields UI elements for actions of any given `ParserGroup` in tabbed sections.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._installTabbedGroupContent
documentation:
Installs a `TabPane` object for given `action` into the `TabbedContent` object with the a matching id.

tabsId: The id of the `TabbedContent` object to install the `TabPane` objects into.
action: The `argparse` action to build from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildActionInputs
documentation:
Yields the UI elements for the given `argparse.Action` objects.

actions: The `argparse.Action` objects to build the UI elements from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildSwitchInput
documentation:
Yields a switch input for the given `action`.

action: The `argparse` action to build from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildDropdownInput
documentation:
Yields a dropdown (select) input for the given `action`.

action: The `argparse` action to build from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._createInput
documentation:
Creates a setup `Input` object for the given `action`.
For the full input group, use `_buildTypedInput(...)`.

action: The `argparse` action to build from.
inputType: The type of input to use for the Textual `Input(type=...)` value.
classes: The classes to add to the input.
value: The value to set the input to initially.
metavarIndex: The index of the `action.metavar` to use for the placeholder when the `action.metavar` is a tuple.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildTypedInput
documentation:
Yields a typed text input group for the given `action`.
For just the `Input` object, use `_createInput(...)`.

action: The `argparse` action to build from.
inputType: The type of input to use for the Textual `Input(type=...)` value.
hideLabel: If `True`, the label will be hidden.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildListInput
documentation:
Yields a list input for the given `action`.

action: The `argparse` action to build from.
showAddRemove: If `True`, the add and remove buttons will be shown with a max count defined by `action.nargs`.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildListInputItem
documentation:
Yields a list input item for the given `action`.

id: The identifier for this list item.
action: The `argparse` action to build from.
value: The initial value for this list item.
showRemove: If `True`, the remove button will be shown for this list item.
metavarIndex: The index of the `action.metavar` to use for the placeholder when the `action.metavar` is a tuple.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._buildSubparserGroup
documentation:
Yields a subparser group for the given `action`.

action: The `argparse` action to build from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._installSubparserGroupContent
documentation:
Installs `TabPane` objects for the given `action` into the `TabbedContent` object with the a matching id.

tabsId: The id of the `TabbedContent` object to install the `TabPane` objects into.
action: The `argparse` action to build from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.getArgs
documentation: Returns the arguments parsed from the interface.
MARK: Functions

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._onlyValidActions
documentation: Gets the valid actions for the given `ArgumentParser` using rules from this Interface.
MARK: Private Functions

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._getValidDests
documentation:
Returns a list of valid destinations for the given `ArgumentParser`.

parser: The parser to get the valid destinations from.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._toTitleCase
documentation: Converts a string to title case.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._splitCamelCase
documentation: Splits a camel case string into words.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._splitSnakeCase
documentation: Splits a snake case string into words.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._codeStrToTitle
documentation: Converts a code stlye string (camelCase or snake_case) to a title case string.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._typedStringToValue
documentation:
Converts a typed input string into an `int`, `float`, the `s` string, or `None`.

s: The string to convert.
inputType: The type of input to convert to.

Returns the converted value.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._exportDOM
documentation: Exports the Textual DOM that is currently displayed.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface._limitString
documentation:
Limits a string to a certain number of characters, adding a postfix if the string is longer than the limit.
Takes the length of the postfix into account.

s: The string to limit.
maxChars: The maximum number of characters the string should have.
postfix: The postfix to add to the string if it is longer than the limit.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.action_onQuit
documentation: Triggers when the user cancels submission and execution.
MARK: Actions

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.action_onSubmit
documentation: Triggers when the user submits the form.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.inputSwitchChanged
documentation: Triggered when an input switch is changed.
MARK: Handlers

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.inputDropdownChanged
documentation: Triggered when an input dropdown is changed.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.inputTypedChanged
documentation: Triggered when a typed text input is changed.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.inputTypedInListChanged

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.listRemoveButtonPressed

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.tabActivated

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.submitButtonPressed
documentation: Triggered when submitting the form.

methods.name: argui.Interface.Interface.navTreeNodeSelected
documentation: Triggered when submitting the form.

Automatic interface for the `argparse` module.

This class is the interface runner for the module.
Use `Wrapper` to automatically handle the interface.
MARK: Classes
